violations.description: General employee cleanliness. — --Food employee did not wash hands as necessary. - Must wash hands between glove changes.Observed dry hand sink at the beinning of inspection.
Preventing contamination during storage. — --Food item - brown sugar and chips - was not stored in a clean, dry location a minimum of 6 inches above the floor.

Reheating food. — --Potentially hazardous food - chili and soup - was not reheated rapidly so all parts reached a temperature of 165F for 15 seconds within one hour. - Chili and soup tempwd between 100-114F. Product had been reheating for about 1 hour; was immediately heated to proper temp of 165F.

Date marking and disposition of ready-to-eat potentially hazardous food. — --Refrigerated, ready-to-eat potentially hazardous food - deli meats and cheese - prepared and packaged by a processing plant was not consumed/discarded within 10 calendar days after original package was opened.--Refrigerated, ready-to-eat potentially hazardous food - quiche -(prepared more than 24 hours ago) was not properly date marked.
